description_long: This is a traditional wooden water well with an octagonal base constructed from vertically aligned wooden planks. The well is topped with a pitched roof made of wooden shingles, supported by two vertical posts and a horizontal crossbeam. Moss or weathering is visible on the roof, suggesting age and outdoor exposure. A wooden bucket with metal bands is placed beside the well, indicating its use for drawing water. The interior of the well is open, and there is no visible cover over the opening. The overall design is rustic and utilitarian, suitable for parks, gardens, or historical urban settings.
description: A rustic wooden water well with an octagonal base, pitched shingle roof, and a bucket for drawing water. Weathered wood and moss details give it an aged, outdoor appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the octagonal wooden well, pitched roof, and central support beam; no bucket is visible from this angle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the wooden roof's planks, the open well, and a wooden bucket placed to the right of the well.
description_view_2: Rear view with a clear look at the roof's structure and a bucket positioned to the left of the well.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the roof's planks and the bucket now on the left side, with the well's open top visible.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A wooden bucket is present beside the well, but no pulley or winding mechanism is visible.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: The well is surrounded by an octagonal wooden wall approximately waist-high, with a pitched wooden roof supported by posts.
